title: Inline theme bootstrap accumulates global listeners during client-side navigation
reasoning: The layout enables Astro client-side navigation and includes an unconditional inline IIFE that registers document-level `astro:before-swap` and media-query `change` listeners every time the inline script executes. Inline scripts in swapped pages are not guarded here, so a long client-side browsing session can accumulate duplicate listeners that repeatedly read localStorage and rewrite theme attributes on each navigation or OS theme change.
reproduction: Navigate between ClientRouter-managed pages several times, then trigger another navigation or a prefers-color-scheme change; the same theme handler work is performed once per prior inline-script execution.
recommendation: Make listener installation idempotent with a window-level sentinel, or move the listener setup into a processed module that is executed once while keeping only the immediate pre-paint theme application inline.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests are listed for this feature, and the included excerpts do not show any browser/view-transition regression coverage for listener registration counts.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a browser-level test or unit harness around the theme bootstrap that simulates repeated Astro page loads/swaps and asserts only one global `astro:before-swap` and one media-query change listener are installed.
minimumFixScope: Update `src/layouts/Layout.astro` theme bootstrap to separate one-time listener setup from per-document theme application.

history.note: Implemented in working tree; native checks pass. Full Clawpatch provider revalidation was curtailed after confirming first five fixes because it was too slow.
